response:
Sec. 1
Original events on November 20, 2020, marked a tragic day when security forces in Lekki, Lagos, used lethal force against peaceful protesters.

Sec. 2
Original footage captured soldiers pointing firearms directly at young people who were marching to demand better governance and economic reforms.

Sec. 3
Original reports from eyewitnesses described how the crowd was initially peaceful before the sudden and unprovoked discharge of live ammunition.

Sec. 4
Original video evidence later circulated globally, showing the terrifying moment when bullets were fired into the air and towards the demonstrators.

Sec. 5
Original investigations by the Nigerian government and international bodies were launched to determine the exact circumstances surrounding the shootings.

Sec. 6
Original families of the victims demanded justice and accountability for the loss of life during the protest at the Lekki toll gate.

Sec. 7
Original memories of that day remain a painful reminder of the need for dialogue and the protection of human rights in Nigeria.
